skill.name: copilot-flow
skill.description: Structures collaborative development work between Claude and Copilot through analysis, design, implementation, review, and delivery stages.
skill.summary: Run a five-stage Claude and Copilot workflow for structured software delivery.
skill.author: Aykahshi

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the reviewed file contains Markdown documentation rather than executable code. The backtick, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance detections are false positives; the real concerns are repository file modification, external Copilot MCP collaboration, and possible interaction history retention.

medium_findings.title: External AI Collaboration May Share Project Context
medium_findings.description: The workflow delegates design and review stages to Copilot through MCP. This is core functionality, but it may expose requirements, code details, or review context to an external assistant depending on the MCP configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly assigns design and review responsibilities to Copilot via MCP. No malicious endpoint is present, so the concern is privacy and data handling rather than confirmed exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Workflow Writes State, Reports, Source Files, and Delivery Artifacts
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to maintain workflow state, produce reports, write source files, and create delivery output. This is legitimate for a development workflow, but users should review changes before applying them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple documented workflow outputs require file system writes. The behavior matches the skill purpose and does not show destructive intent.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Matches Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings map to Markdown inline code, code fences, output filenames, and slash command examples. No executable script, shell invocation, or command interpolation was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows documentation examples and filenames inside Markdown formatting. There is no Ruby code, shell code, or executable wrapper in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Matches Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-risk weak cryptography detections appear to match ordinary documentation text such as design, workflow descriptions, or .md filenames. No cryptographic API, hashing function, or encryption operation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain prose and Markdown output names, not code using MD5, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms. The scanner likely matched substrings in natural language.

low_findings.title: Static Network Reconnaissance Match Is a Documentation False Positive
low_findings.description: The low-risk network reconnaissance finding points to workflow state documentation. No scanning command, host discovery logic, or network probing instruction was found at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 86 documents a local workflow-state file path. It does not include a reconnaissance tool name or a network operation.

content.value_statement: Complex coding tasks often lose structure across planning, implementation, and review. This skill defines a staged Claude and Copilot workflow with clear artifacts.
actual_capabilities: Defines a five-stage workflow: analyze, design, implement, review, and deliver.
Documents slash commands for running the full flow or individual stages.
Assigns analysis, implementation, and delivery work to Claude.
Assigns architecture design and code review work to Copilot through MCP.
Specifies expected artifacts such as analysis reports, design notes, review reports, and delivery packages.
Describes workflow state recovery through a local state file.
limitations: The skill file is documentation and does not include the command implementations.
Copilot MCP and claude-mem must be configured separately.
The workflow can modify project files when implementation stages run.
Users must review outputs for correctness, security, and project fit.

use_cases.title: Plan a Feature Build
use_cases.description: Break a feature request into analysis, architecture, implementation, review, and final delivery steps.

use_cases.title: Add Independent Review
use_cases.description: Use Copilot review stages to check code quality, security, performance, and best practices after Claude implements changes.

use_cases.title: Recover Interrupted Work
use_cases.description: Resume a multi-stage task from documented workflow state and previously generated stage artifacts.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Full Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Run copilot-flow for this task: build a small user profile editing feature. Show the planned stages before making changes.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Requirements Only
prompt_templates.scenario: Gather structure before design or implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the analyze stage for this request: improve our password reset flow. Produce clear requirements and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Request Architecture Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Ask Copilot to design from existing analysis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the design stage with Copilot MCP. Create architecture guidance for the analyzed notification service changes.

prompt_templates.title: Run Review and Delivery
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use after implementation is complete.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the review stage for the recent API changes, then prepare delivery notes with risks, tests, and follow-up work.

output_examples.input: Run copilot-flow to implement a JWT authentication API.
output_examples.output: A staged delivery plan with analysis notes, architecture recommendations, implementation work, review findings, and final delivery documentation.

output_examples.input: Review the authentication module security using copilot-flow.
output_examples.output: A focused review report covering security concerns, code quality observations, performance notes, and recommended fixes.
A status summary that identifies the last completed stage and the next stage to continue.

best_practices: Start with clear requirements and constraints before running the full workflow.
Review each generated artifact before allowing the next stage to change files.
Use individual stages for focused analysis, design, implementation, or review tasks.
anti_patterns: Do not run the implementation stage on repositories with uncommitted critical work.
Do not send confidential code or data to Copilot MCP without policy approval.
Do not skip review when the workflow changes authentication, security, or data handling code.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It defines a staged workflow that coordinates Claude and Copilot for software analysis, design, implementation, review, and delivery.

faq.question: Does it include executable code?
faq.answer: No. The scanned skill contains Markdown instructions and command documentation, not executable scripts.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The static alerts are false positives, but the workflow can change files and may share project context with Copilot MCP.

faq.question: Can I run only one stage?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents individual analyze, design, implement, review, and deliver stage commands.

faq.question: What should I configure first?
faq.answer: Configure Copilot MCP and any memory integration before relying on design, review, or history features.

faq.question: How should teams use it safely?
faq.answer: Use version control, review generated files, and avoid sending confidential material to external tools without approval.
